auc: 0.701 (aided case-level ROC for Gleason ≥6, all cases)
intended_use: Prostate MR AI is a plug-in Radiological Computer Assisted Detection and Diagnosis Software device intended to be used with a separate hosting application as a concurrent reading aid to assist radiolo
intended_use_full: Prostate MR AI is a plug-in Radiological Computer Assisted Detection and Diagnosis Software device intended to be used with a separate hosting application as a concurrent reading aid to assist radiologists in the interpretation of a prostate MRI examination acquired according to the PI-RADS standard in adult men (40 years and older) with suspected cancer in treatment naïve prostate glands. The plug-in software analyzes non-contrast T2 weighted (T2W) and diffusion weighted image (DWI) series to segment the prostate gland and to provide an automatic detection and segmentation of regions suspicious for cancer. For each suspicious region detected, the algorithm moreover provides a lesion Score, by way of PI-RADS interpretation suggestion.
target_condition: Prostate cancer (suspected, in treatment-naïve prostate glands)
ground_truth: Biopsy results (pathology), consensus radiologist readings (radiology), and negative MRI with ≥12 months PSA or MRI follow-up
other_metrics: Case-level ROC AUC unaided: 0.676 (all cases), 0.658 (biopsy/follow-up cases); Mean difference aided vs unaided: +0.025 (all cases, 95% CI [0.001, 0.049]), +0.037 (biopsy cases, 95% CI [0.011, 0.063]). Lesion-level wAFROC unaided: 0.734 (all cases), 0.772 (biopsy cases); aided: 0.769 (all cases), 0.834 (biopsy cases); mean difference: +0.035 (all cases, 95% CI [0.002, 0.068]), +0.030 (biopsy cases, 95% CI [0.008, 0.052]). Prostate segmentation: median Dice score >0.9; normalized volume difference ±5%. Lesion detection case-level sensitivity ≥0.80 (radiology and pathology ground truth); false positive rate <1 per case.
